mechanism: Topical polysaccharides (for example acemannan) support moist wound healing and anti-inflammatory signaling; anthraquinone-rich latex acts as a stimulant laxative.
safetyNotes: Topical gel is usually well tolerated; oral latex can cause cramping, diarrhea, and clinically significant potassium loss. Avoid internal latex use during pregnancy, breastfeeding, bowel disease, or kidney disease.

description: Laxative, wound healing, skin soother. Herbs often act via neurotransmitter modulation, antioxidant effects, or enzyme inhibition.. None. ; nan; nan.
interactions: Latex may increase potassium loss with loop or thiazide diuretics and can raise digoxin toxicity risk.
Latex can potentiate hypokalemia with corticosteroids or licorice use.
Latex laxative effect may alter absorption of oral medicines if taken at the same time.
legalStatus: Approved for external use; oral use regulated
